attack_surface.data_description: $BGT balance checking or 1:1 redemption logic in BeraHub smart contracts

security_gap.gap_description: A flaw in BeraHub's smart contracts could allow a user to redeem more $BGT than they own, or to receive $BERA at a ratio more favorable than 1:1.

actor_risk.description: A malicious $BGT Holder (Actor 4) could exploit a contract flaw to unfairly acquire more $BERA than entitled during redemption.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting redemption logic to gain excess $BERA can disrupt tokenomics and cause financial loss to the system.

assumptions: The BeraHub smart contracts for $BGT redemption contain a bug in balance checking or the 1:1 conversion logic.
This bug is exploitable by a $BGT Holder.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorous security audits of BeraHub's redemption smart contracts, focusing on balance management and the 1:1 conversion mechanism. Formal verification if feasible.

description: A malicious $BGT Holder (Actor 4) exploits a vulnerability in the BeraHub (Component 106) smart contracts to redeem more $BGT than they legitimately own, or to receive $BERA at a rate greater than the intended 1:1 ratio.
